model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Michigan is the pick because the Wolverines still own a major full-season quality edge, ranking No. 2 nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with elite efficiency on both ends, while Georgia profiles more like a fringe top-30 team with a much shakier defense. Georgia has a credible upset path because its offense is strong enough to make this a live game if it wins the shot-making battle, but the Bulldogs’ defensive numbers and overall efficiency gap make that path narrower. Michigan’s season-ending loss of L.J. Cason affects depth, yet it is not enough to outweigh the Wolverines’ superior two-way profile and higher floor.

reasoningStep.title: Michigan over Georgia
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan’s two-way efficiency profile is substantially stronger, and Georgia’s defensive vulnerability makes the upset less likely despite the Bulldogs’ offensive punch.
evidence: Michigan ratings: KenPom No. 2 (AdjEM 38.38, AdjO 126.7 No. 8, AdjD 88.4), Torvik No. 2 with a 31-2 record and top-10 offense/top-2 defense.
Georgia ratings: KenPom No. 31 (AdjEM 20.47, AdjO 124.7 No. 15, AdjD 104.2), Torvik No. 37 with offense ranked No. 18 but defense No. 93.
Availability check: Michigan guard L.J. Cason is out for the season with a torn ACL, but no more significant Michigan absence surfaced to materially change the matchup.
Underdog path: Georgia can threaten if its offense drives a high-efficiency shooting game, but its weaker defense gives Michigan a clearer and more reliable path over 40 minutes.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
